label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out the answer to the question: The car tested by Oxford University are different from others in that they _ . The choices are (A) use available low-cost technology, (B) depend on GPS to understand the location, (C) can choose the places for their owners to visit, or (D) can develop a good relationship with their owners. First, let me read through the passage again and see what's said about the Oxford car compared to others. The first paragraph says that the car is different from those being tested by Audi, Ford, etc. It's much cheaper because it's based on off-the-shelf technology and controlled by an ordinary iPad. So "off-the-shelf" means available, not custom-made, and low-cost. That would support option A. Also, it mentions that instead of using GPS to understand location, the car learns routes when a person is driving. So that rules out B as a correct answer because the Oxford car doesn't use GPS. Looking at the other options: C says the car can choose the places for their owners to visit. The passage says the car learns routes to places the driver frequents, like work, the pub, or grocery store. But the car doesn't choose the places; it just learns the routes the driver already takes. So C is incorrect. D says the car can develop a good relationship with the owners. The passage doesn't mention anything about the car forming relationships. It's more about functionality. So D is not supported. So based on the
